Title: Towards novel herbicide modes of action by inhibiting lysine biosynthesis in plants

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Lysine biosynthesis via the DAP pathway
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as the first and rate-limiting step in lysine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses L-aspartate semialdehyde and pyruvate to form the cyclic product of the first committed step toward dihydrodipicolinate in lysine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies the first committed precursor and controls flux toward lysine
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: DHDPS enzyme; allosterically regulated lysine-biosynthesis enzyme; plant chloroplast homotetramer
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Essential amino acid; crop nutritional quality target; pathway inhibition yields herbicidal phytotoxicity
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Chemical inhibition of AtDHDPS1 reduces Arabidopsis germination and seedling/soil growth by blocking lysine-biosynthesis flux
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Recombinant enzyme inhibition assays with IC50 determination; X-ray co-crystallography with inhibitor MBDTA-2; Arabidopsis seedling agar assays; soil pre-emergence herbicidal assays
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Treatment with MBDTA-1 or MBDTA-2; agar seedling assay; soil pre-emergence conditions
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtDHDPS1 was a primary biochemical target in a high-throughput screen that identified the first plant DHDPS inhibitors. 2. MBDTA-1 and MBDTA-2 inhibited recombinant AtDHDPS1 with IC50 values of 126 ± 6.50 μM and 63.3 ± 1.80 μM, respectively. 3. Co-crystal structure showed MBDTA-2 binds a novel allosteric pocket adjacent to the lysine-binding site. 4. Inhibitor treatment strongly impaired Arabidopsis germination and growth, supporting DHDPS inhibition as a herbicidal mode of action.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: AtDHDPS1 is more sensitive than AtDHDPS2 to lysine in prior work, but MBDTA compounds showed similar inhibition of both isoforms in this study. The MBDTA-2 binding pocket is highly conserved across monocot and dicot plant DHDPS proteins. PDB structure of AtDHDPS1 with MBDTA-2: 7MDS.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Inhibition of this key lysine-biosynthesis step is lethal/phytotoxic; potential cascading metabolic shifts such as threonine accumulation are discussed but not tested here
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A strong herbicide target for developing novel pre-emergence herbicides. Crop tolerance may be engineered through inhibitor-insensitive mutant DHDPS alleles because the inhibitor binds an allosteric rather than active site; could be combined with other herbicides for synergistic resistance management.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Lysine biosynthesis via the DAP pathway
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as the first and rate-limiting step in lysine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses L-aspartate semialdehyde and pyruvate to form the cyclic product of the first committed step toward dihydrodipicolinate in lysine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies the first committed precursor and controls flux toward lysine
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: DHDPS enzyme; allosterically regulated lysine-biosynthesis enzyme; plant chloroplast homotetramer
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Essential amino acid; crop nutritional quality target; pathway inhibition yields herbicidal phytotoxicity
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Chemical inhibition of AtDHDPS2 supports suppression of lysine-biosynthesis capacity associated with Arabidopsis growth inhibition
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Recombinant enzyme inhibition assays with IC50 determination; inhibitor comparison across both Arabidopsis isoforms
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Treatment with MBDTA-1 or MBDTA-2 in biochemical assays
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtDHDPS2 was directly tested as a second Arabidopsis lysine-biosynthesis target isoform. 2. MBDTA-1 and MBDTA-2 inhibited recombinant AtDHDPS2 with IC50 values of 116 ± 5.20 μM and 64.0 ± 1.00 μM, respectively. 3. Similar potency against both isoforms indicates the new inhibitor class broadly targets plant DHDPS enzymes.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: AtDHDPS2 required GroEL/ES chaperone complex for correct folding during heterologous expression. Although structural work focused on AtDHDPS1, binding-pocket residues are conserved in AtDHDPS2.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: As with DHDPS1, inhibition is expected to cause phytotoxicity and possibly broader amino-acid homeostasis disturbances
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important paralog to consider when engineering crop tolerance, because both plant isoforms may need to be rendered inhibitor-insensitive for robust herbicide selectivity.
